figures.caption: Fig. 8. Qualitative comparison results of compound actions combining ’throwing’ with either ’walking’, ’running’ or ’jumping’. Later frames are rendered with increased transparency for clarity, and arrows indicate motion direction.

Fig. 6. Qualitative comparison of compound actions combining ’greeting’ with ’walking’ or ’running’. Later frames are rendered with increased transparency for clarity, and arrows indicate motion direction.

Fig. 7. Qualitative comparison results of compound actions combining ’stretching’ with either ’hopping’ or ’walking’. Later frames are rendered with increased transparency for clarity, and arrows indicate motion direction.

Fig. 1. Motion sequences generated by our Motion-Adapter given a textual prompt and a pre-trained motion diffusion model (e.g., MDM [1]). The MotionAdapter MDM more accurately captures compound actions compared with existing models. Later frames are rendered with increased transparency for clarity, and arrows indicate the direction of displacement.

Fig. 2. Comparison of attention maps from SALAD [3] and our MotionAdapter. SALAD’s maps are averaged across all Transformer layers at step 40, while ours are taken from the third cross-attention layer at step 750.

TABLE IV QUANTITATIVE RESULTS OF ABLATION STUDIES. SYMBOLS: ↑ (HIGHER IS BETTER), ↓ (LOWER IS BETTER), AND → (CLOSER TO THE REAL DISTRIBUTION IS BETTER). EACH METHOD WAS EVALUATED 20 TIMES; ± INDICATES THE 95% CONFIDENCE INTERVAL. BEST RESULTS ARE IN BOLD.
Fig. 3. Overview of the Motion-Adapter integrated into the diffusion model at step t.

Fig. 10. Qualitative comparison results of compound actions with complex textual prompts. To enhance visual clarity, the later frames are rendered with increased transparency, and arrows indicate the direction of motion.

TABLE II QUANTITATIVE RESULTS ON THE TEST SETS. SYMBOLS: ↑ (HIGHER IS BETTER), ↓ (LOWER IS BETTER), AND → (CLOSER TO THE REAL DISTRIBUTION IS BETTER). EACH METHOD WAS EVALUATED 20 TIMES; ± INDICATES THE 95% CONFIDENCE INTERVAL. THE BEST SCORES ARE SHOWN IN BOLD, WHILE THE SECOND-BEST SCORES ARE UNDERLINED.
Fig. 11. Results of our Motion-Adapter MotionDiffuse.
TABLE I UPPER-BODY AND LOWER-BODY MOTION CAPTIONS OF OUR BENCHMARK.
